title: Protein Expression of Hard-to-Propose Proteins in E. coli Cytoplasm
author: Cristina Hernandez Rollan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfbwjipe

steps.instruction: Pick a fresh colony of your BL21 (DE3) strain previously transformed with your expression vector, and inoculate it in LB medium supplemented with relevant antibiotics. Grow the culture at 37 °C at 250 RPM shaking Overnight . The volume of the overnight culture depends on the volume of the expression culture and should be at least 1/100 of the expression culture
Dilute the overnight culture 1:100 in fresh LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics
Collect induced samples by centrifugation at 8000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes and carefully discard the supernatant Pellets can be frozen here at -80 °C or -20 °C until protein extraction
Grow the culture at 37 °C with 250 RPM shaking until an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6
Induce the expression by adding IPTG to a final concentration of 1 millimolar (mM)
Grow the culture for 20:00:00 at 18 °C with 180 RPM shacking. The temperature can be lowered further allowing for a slower induction in order to increase the production of soluble product.
Lyse the cells using a lysis protocol or a commercially available kit
Keep the extractions On ice when working with it and at 4 °C for storage
Move the culture into an incubator set to 18 °C with 180 RPM of shaking and allow the culture to reach OD 600 = 0.8 - 1.0
